steps.title: Find the shared input $c$
steps.content: Since $p$ has slope $6$, write $p(x)=6x+b$. Use $p(4)=28$: $$6(4)+b=28,$$ so $b=4$. Then solve $p(c)=-8$: $$6c+4=-8,$$ so $c=-2$.

steps.title: Find the slope of $t$
steps.content: Now $t(c)=-7$ becomes $t(-2)=-7$, and the other point is $t(4)=35$. The slope of $t$ is $$\frac{35-(-7)}{4-(-2)}=\frac{42}{6}=7.$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The slope of $y=t(x)$ is $7$, which matches the correct answer. The hand calculation agrees: $\dfrac{35-(-7)}{4-(-2)}=\dfrac{42}{6}=7$.
